articles.title: Robert Parish: Talk of Celtics trading Jaylen Brown is ‘disturbing’
articles.description:
“It would be a serious miscalculation on Brad Stevens, management and ownership."

The post Robert Parish: Talk of Celtics trading Jaylen Brown is ‘disturbing’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Celtics legend Robert Parish criticizes trade rumors about Jaylen Brown as 'disturbing'.
Parish warns it would be a serious miscalculation by Celtics management and ownership.
Local fans express concern over potential impact on team dynamics and future success.

articles.title: State Police sergeant, former lieutenant under criminal investigation for alleged coverup in wake of fatal crash, court documents reveal
articles.description:
The former lieutenant was previously named in an independent investigation released by the Middlesex District Attorney’s Office.

summary: State Police sergeant and former lieutenant face criminal investigation for alleged coverup after fatal crash.
Middlesex District Attorney's Office previously named the former lieutenant in an independent investigation.
Investigation may impact public trust in local law enforcement and accountability in Boston.

articles.title: Red Sox’ 5-game winning streak snapped in 8-1 loss to Nationals
articles.description:
Red Sox starting pitcher Connelly Early left the game after the fourth inning with left elbow discomfort.

The post Red Sox’ 5-game winning streak snapped in 8-1 loss to Nationals appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Red Sox lost 8-1 to the Nationals, ending their 5-game winning streak.
Starting pitcher Connelly Early exited after four innings due to elbow discomfort.
This loss may affect the team's momentum as they head into upcoming games.

Local fans expect enhanced competitiveness in the upcoming NHL season.

articles.title: Watch: Red Sox, Nationals brawl after Willson Contreras charges mound
articles.description:

Local fans express concern over rising tensions in MLB games affecting Boston's sports culture.

articles.title: Person shot and killed, suspect also dead after incident that closed I-495 in Lowell for hours, officials say
articles.description:
The police activity resulted in both the northbound and southbound lanes of I-495 being closed Tuesday afternoon.

The post Person shot and killed, suspect also dead after incident that closed I-495 in Lowell for hours, officials say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Police responded to a shooting incident on I-495 in Lowell on Tuesday afternoon.
Both northbound and southbound lanes of I-495 were closed for several hours.
Local traffic was significantly disrupted, affecting commuters and residents in the area.

articles.title: Dunkin’ drops new ‘Eagle Cup’ for America’s 250th
articles.description:
Nothing says “America” quite like drinking ice coffee from a bald eagle’s head.

The cup features a design inspired by a bald eagle, symbolizing patriotism.
Boston residents can purchase the limited edition cup starting July 4, 2026.

articles.title: 3 injured in Theater District shooting
articles.description:
The victims all had non-life-threatening injuries, police say.

summary: Three individuals sustained non-life-threatening injuries in a Theater District shooting on June 30.

Local businesses may face reduced foot traffic due to heightened fears of violence.

articles.title: Healey vows urgent response as Haverhill sewage spill fouls Merrimack River
articles.description:
“I think that will, essentially, save the Fourth of July from ruin," Haverhill Mayor Melinda Barrett said.

The post Healey vows urgent response as Haverhill sewage spill fouls Merrimack River appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Haverhill experienced a sewage spill affecting the Merrimack River on June 30.
Governor Maura Healey pledged an urgent response to mitigate the environmental damage.
Mayor Melinda Barrett stated this action is crucial to preserve Fourth of July celebrations.
